name: Twin Higgs
summary: A Z_2 mirror symmetry exchanges the SM with a 'twin' SM. The Higgs becomes a pseudo-Goldstone boson of an approximate global SU(4) under which the Z_2 acts; one-loop quadratic corrections to the visible Higgs mass cancel against twin-sector loops. The twin partners are SM gauge singlets, evading direct LHC constraints — 'neutral naturalness'.

addresses_puzzles.note: Cancels the dominant top-quark contribution to Higgs mass at one loop without coloured top partners. Two-loop sensitivity remains; viable parameter space requires twin sector mass scale ~ few hundred GeV.

Twin-baryons and twin-electroweak states can be cosmologically stable and gravitationally interacting; specific realisations (Fraternal Twin Higgs, Mirror Twin Higgs with asymmetric reheating) give viable DM candidates.

touches_benchmarks.note: Mixing of the SM Higgs with the twin Higgs reduces SM-Higgs coupling strengths by a factor cos^2(theta) = v^2/f^2. Current Higgs coupling measurements bound v/f >~ 3, requiring a mild tuning.
Twin photons and twin neutrinos contribute to N_eff; mirror models without asymmetric reheating violate CMB+BBN bounds, motivating asymmetric-temperature variants.

new_fields: full mirror SM sector (twin quarks, leptons, gauge bosons, Higgs)
introduces.note: Z_2 fixes most parameters to be twins of SM parameters; the new free inputs are the breaking scale f, the explicit Z_2 breaking, and a small handful of scalar-potential terms.

history: Chacko, Goh, Harnik (2006). Orbifold Higgs / neutral naturalness systematics (Craig, Knapen, Longhi 2014, arXiv:1410.6808) generalised the construction; Fraternal Twin Higgs (Craig, Katz, Strassler, Sundrum 2015, arXiv:1501.05310) addresses cosmological problems by removing twin light fermions. Active research direction in 2015-present.

references.citation: Chacko, Goh, Harnik, 'The Twin Higgs: Natural electroweak breaking from mirror symmetry', PRL 96, 231802 (2006)
Craig, Knapen, Longhi, 'Neutral Naturalness from the Orbifold Higgs', JHEP 03 (2015) 097
